summary.intent: Push a commit to main and validate it passes local CI checks.
summary.outcome: Commit 07cc1a3b was pushed, but biome formatting checks failed on 3 JS files; a follow-up commit was made to fix the formatting.

repo: The project uses biome for JS formatting with strict line-length rules — long string literals in locale files and ternary expressions in template literals must be broken across multiple lines.
local-validate.sh runs parallel checks (biome, fmt, i18n, install-names, install-docs, zizmor) and reports per-check pass/fail with timing.
On macOS without nvcc, local-validate.sh forces non-CUDA validation and skips --all-features; override via LOCAL_VALIDATE_* env vars.
llama-cpp-sys-2 build dirs are cleaned from both debug and release targets at the start of each local validation run.

code.finding: testProviderConfirm string was too long for biome's line limit; must be split with key on its own line and value indented on the next.
Same testProviderConfirm line-length issue as the en locale.
Inline ternary expressions inside tagged template literals (html`...`) must be expanded: condition on its own line, branches indented, closing brace on its own line.

workflow: Running local-validate.sh after pushing (rather than before) caused a second fix commit; running it pre-push would catch formatting issues earlier.
Biome auto-fix (biome format --write) resolves formatting errors cleanly without touching unrelated code.

friction: Biome formatting failures were only discovered after the commit was already pushed, requiring a second cleanup commit on main.
Multiple stale llama-cpp-sys-2 build directories accumulated in both debug and release targets, adding noise to validation output.
open_items: Consider adding a pre-push git hook or pre-commit biome check to catch formatting issues before they land on main.
Biome reported pre-existing complexity lint warnings (not treated as errors) — these were intentionally left unfixed and may warrant a future cleanup pass.
